original_hypothesis.title: GTC test token transferFrom() ignores allowances, enabling unauthorized transfers if accidentally used in production deployments
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: In the test_mocks/GTC.sol implementation, transferFrom() explicitly removes the allowance check ('Removed allowance check for easy testing'). If this token (or a similar misconfigured token) is used in a real deployment, any caller could transfer tokens from any address, enabling immediate theft.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The project states GTC.sol is test-only, but accidental deployment or wrong address wiring is a known real-world failure mode. This becomes catastrophic if IdentityStaking or other systems accept this token as the stake token.
deployment_assumptions: The test_mocks/GTC.sol token (or an equivalent allowance-bypassing token) is deployed on a target network and used as the staking token or otherwise holds user funds
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: Repository includes test_mocks/GTC.sol; no provided deployment scripts show IdentityStaking initialization with canonical production GTC address.

proof_of_signal.pattern: transferFrom(): // Removed allowance check for easy testing

preconditions: This GTC.sol (test mock) is used as a live token holding value, or is configured as IdentityStaking.token
Victim addresses hold balances in this token
Attacker can call transferFrom(victim, attacker, amount)

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: unauthorized token theft from arbitrary holders

analysis_questions: Are there safeguards in deployment tooling preventing test mocks from being deployed to production networks?
Is there any address allowlist for tokens accepted by IdentityStaking deployments?
Could the same code path exist in any forked token on target chains?
validation_requirements: Search deployment pipelines/CI for protections (network checks, artifact exclusions)
Verify on target chains that production uses canonical GTC address
Add tests/lints preventing linking test_mocks into production builds

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: If the mock token is live, attacker calls transferFrom(victim, attacker, victimBalance) with no approval required, draining accounts immediately.
prerequisites: Operational mistake: mock token deployed/used with real value

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Total loss of all token balances held by users/contract
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All holders of the deployed mock token; potentially all stakers if used for staking
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: No on-chain recovery; requires migration and incident response

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: N/A (this is explicitly test-only), but missing build/deploy guardrails is the gap

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: On-chain: any account can drain any other without approvals

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
